taskDescription: Arrange the steps for reducing a dislocated temporomandibular joint (TMJ) using the Hippocratic manoeuvre in the correct order.
paragraphs: Seat the patient on a low stool with the head supported against a wall or headrest.
Stand in front of the patient and place both thumbs (wrapped in gauze) on the lower molar teeth on each side.
Wrap the remaining fingers under the chin to grip the mandible firmly.
Press the thumbs downward to depress the posterior part of the mandible, overcoming the spasm of the lateral pterygoid muscle.
Guide the mandible backward so the condyle slides back into the mandibular fossa of the temporal bone.

overallFeedback.feedback: Keep studying — review the material and try again.

Excellent work! You have a strong grasp of this topic.
